faqs.q: How is fatigue different from normal tiredness?
faqs.a: Ordinary tiredness after a busy day usually eases with rest, while fatigue is persistent and out of proportion to activity. The defining difference is that it lingers and isn't fully relieved by sleep.

faqs.q: Can fatigue be a sign of depression?
faqs.a: Yes. Fatigue is a common feature of depression and anxiety, and it can also follow ongoing stress, poor sleep, or burnout. Fatigue alongside low mood or loss of interest may point to depression.

faqs.q: Does fatigue always come from a mental health condition?
faqs.a: No. It has many medical causes too, including anemia, thyroid problems, infections, chronic illnesses, and medication effects. Because the list is long, clinicians often look at the whole picture rather than assuming a single cause.
